title: Support classes that implement `__call__`
body:
## Summary

This looked straightforward and removes some TODOs.
author: charliermarsh

### Linter (preview)

review_comments.body: This is just `Type::Class(_instance_ty)`. Should I do that directly, or do we prefer to go through `to_meta_type`?

I think here you can just do it directly! Maybe add a comment saying that because it's a dunder it's important to access it as an attribute on the class rather than the instance, though (matching the runtime semantics)

there are some existing bugs where we don't do this elsewhere (that we should fix), but it's actually somewhat important: because we're accessing `__call__` as an attribute on the class rather than the instance, `__call__` is actually a "pure function" here rather than a method. That means that we need to manually insert `self` into the `arg_types` we're passing into the `.call()` method here.

It doesn't make a difference right now, but it will when we start validating the types of arguments passed into typed functions.

I guess something like this?

```suggestion
                    &dunder_call_method.call(db, std::iter::once(self).chain(arg_types).collect::<Vec<_>>())
```

We may want to better optimize the way we pass around call args in the future to avoid this re-allocation (the runtime has the same issue!) but I think for now it's fine to just get the args right, it'll be easier to see how to optimize it once we have more cases in place, and are actually validating args.

> there are some existing bugs where we don't do this elsewhere (that we should fix)

(I fixed the other bugs in #13593!)
Easy to avoid the allocation here later.

label_notes: Third charliermarsh/ruff accepted case capturing 'small straightforward founder PR' sub-pattern. charliermarsh (prior_prs=999, MEMBER, Astral co-founder) submitted +45/-2 PR implementing __call__ dunder protocol for class instances in red_knot. Body extremely terse: 'This looked straightforward and removes some TODOs' — appropriate for established-author small-scope work. Merged ~13h later. 9 review comments (smaller scale than #13562's 25+ and #13579's 28). Methodologically distinct contributions: (1) AlexWaygood teaches runtime-semantics distinction — 'because we're accessing call as an attribute on the class rather than the instance, call is actually a pure function here rather than a method. That means that we need to manually insert self into the arg_types' — quality-indicator signal that reviewers explicitly clarify runtime model adherence; (2) carljm pragmatic-not-perfect philosophy — 'We may want to better optimize... but I think for now it's fine to just get the args right' — calibration that quality-merged PRs don't require performance-optimality; (3) AlexWaygood cross-references parallel PR — '(I fixed the other bugs in #13593!)' — shows active multi-PR engineering rhythm in same area; (4) charlie's forward-link to #13595 indicates follow-up commitment continued. Same-day parallel work: charlie merged #13562 (Sep 30 20:50), then on Oct 1 merged this PR (17:15) and #13579 (18:04) — three substantive type-inference PRs in 22h. Test coverage: one focused test covering both positive (Multiplier with call) and negative (Unit without) paths plus diagnostic assertion. Calibration value: critic must recognize quality-merged PRs span a range of scales — small terse PRs from established authors are equally valid as large multi-reviewer ones; quality is not just review-volume but appropriateness-to-scope. SATURATION NOTICE: 3/3 accepted cases are now charliermarsh+ruff. HARD CEILING enforced going forward — no further charliermarsh+ruff accepted cases unless completely new sub-pattern (e.g., contested merge, cross-team collaboration, review-driven rewrite, controversial design decision). Next accepted targets MUST be different authors and/or different repos. Anchor priorities remain: HA#160608 (farkasdi successful split-merge), ghostty#12518 (disclosed-AI merge), and 17 diverse cases.
